title: PEER SUPPORT - 211info
text: SALEM DROP PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help, advice, guidance, and support from peer support specialists who have personal experience in youth-serving systems, including mental health, foster care, and juvenile justice. Services are community-based and can take place at individuals' homes or other location in the community, depending on the needs and preferences of the individual. 246 State Street Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 12-25 Hours: Varies by service Email Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Completed application AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Coos, Jackson, Lane, and Marion Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 3pm-6pm Services: Age Peer Networking Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY FACILITIES/CENTERS - DROP-IN CENTER If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. SALEM DROP
